meta.description: Curated publication annotations and featured flags. These are merged with live ORCID data by index.html at runtime. Match publications to ORCID records via doi or putCode.

featured.annotation: A conceptual framework paper making the case that teaching evolution as an interdisciplinary science — spanning biology, psychology, anthropology, and sustainability — significantly expands its educational potential beyond traditional bio-only treatments.

A design-based research study documenting how we built and tested a pre-service teacher education module for primary schools — one of few empirical studies on evolution teacher education below the secondary level.

Connects behavioral science — particularly work on cognitive biases, social norms, and cooperation — to the competency frameworks of Education for Sustainable Development, arguing for metacognition as a bridging construct.

Introduces causal mapping as a classroom tool for helping students navigate the multiple and interacting causes of human behavioral evolution — addressing a persistent misconception challenge in evolution education.

annotations.note: Systematic textbook analysis — finds that cooperation is significantly underrepresented as a causal factor in German high school biology, despite its centrality in current evolutionary science.
Peer commentary in Behavioral and Brain Sciences — situating the music-bonding hypothesis within multilevel selection theory.
Book chapter outlining the Community Science Lab model — a school improvement framework that uses evolutionary and behavioral science as a conceptual toolkit for participatory school development.
Companion chapter focusing on pedagogy: how to sequence and scaffold interdisciplinary evolutionary concepts across subject areas and age groups.
Chapter in a collaborative European volume on socioscientific issues — our contribution focuses on common pool resource games and agent-based models as evolution education tools.
